edges.description: Reduced DNMT3A activity perturbs developmental methylation and transcriptional programs.
DNMT3A dysfunction can disrupt cardiovascular development and maintenance in a subset of patients.
Altered developmental methylation programs contribute to the overgrowth phenotype.
Epigenetic dysregulation contributes to the neurodevelopmental phenotype.
Cardiovascular involvement can manifest with ventricular arrhythmias.

nodes.description: DNMT3A dysfunction perturbs developmental epigenetic regulation and contributes to the growth and neurodevelopmental phenotype of TBRS.

evidence.reference_title: Aortic disease and cardiomyopathy in patients with a novel DNMT3A gene variant causing Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome
evidence.snippet: This highlights the importance of epigenetic regulation by DNMT3A on cardiovascular system development and function.

nodes.description: Arrhythmias are part of the expanded cardiovascular phenotype in some TBRS families.

evidence.reference_title: Aortic disease and cardiomyopathy in patients with a novel DNMT3A gene variant causing Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome
evidence.snippet: The affected family members (mother and brother) were diagnosed with MV regurgitation, LV dilatation, and arrhythmias.

nodes.description: Some pathogenic DNMT3A variants are associated with aortic disease, cardiomyopathy, and arrhythmias, expanding the disease mechanism beyond the core overgrowth phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Aortic disease and cardiomyopathy in patients with a novel DNMT3A gene variant causing Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome
evidence.snippet: A 34-year-old proband presented with progressive aortic dilatation, mitral valve (MV) regurgitation, left ventricular (LV) dilatation, and ventricular arrhythmias.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the genetic and clinical spectrum of Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome in a series of 24 French patients
evidence.snippet: This syndrome is characterised by overgrowth, intellectual disability and distinctive facial features and is the consequence of germline loss-of-function variants in DNMT3A
DNMT3A | HGNC:2978 | Tatton-Brown-Rahman overgrowth syndrome | MONDO:0014382 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Germline pathogenic DNMT3A variants reduce normal methyltransferase function, establishing the primary molecular lesion in Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the genetic and clinical spectrum of Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome in a series of 24 French patients
evidence.snippet: This syndrome is characterised by overgrowth, intellectual disability and distinctive facial features and is the consequence of germline loss-of-function variants in DNMT3A

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is a universal or near-universal feature of TBRS.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the genetic and clinical spectrum of Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome in a series of 24 French patients
evidence.snippet: We confirmed that the main phenotypic features were intellectual disability (100% of individuals), distinctive facial features (96%) and overgrowth (87%).

nodes.description: Overgrowth is one of the defining and most common features of Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the genetic and clinical spectrum of Tatton-Brown-Rahman syndrome in a series of 24 French patients
evidence.snippet: We confirmed that the main phenotypic features were intellectual disability (100% of individuals), distinctive facial features (96%) and overgrowth (87%).
